text.title: RAG Canary Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes specific to RAG canary rollouts. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Slow kill-switch — the 60-second rollback contract is violated because the gateway requires a deploy or cache warm-up; quality regressions persist for tens of minutes.
failure-mode.detector: rollback_receipt.completed_within_seconds > 60 OR rollback requires CI pipeline run
failure-mode.repair: Redesign the gateway for atomic version flips (in-memory routing table, hot-reload). Quarterly game-day rehearses the rollback; any failure to meet the 60s contract is a feature-blocking engineering task.

failure-mode.description: Sampling too thin — online quality scoring runs at 0.5% of traffic, the signal lags 24h behind a quality regression, kill-switch fires too late.
failure-mode.detector: plan.online_quality.sample_rate < 0.02 OR samples per hour < 200 during the 5% step
failure-mode.repair: Raise sample rate to at least 5% during canary; absolute floor of 200 samples/hour. Cost-control via judge-batching and prompt caching, not by lowering the sample rate.

failure-mode.description: Eval set stale — golden eval has not been updated for 6 months, no longer represents current user questions, eval pass at the gate gives false confidence.
failure-mode.detector: golden eval refreshed_at older than 90 days OR eval-set composition drift (new question patterns in production that the set lacks) > 30%
failure-mode.repair: Refresh golden eval before every rollout. Pull fresh questions from production samples; review with a domain expert; commit the new version. The eval is only as good as its representativeness.

failure-mode.description: LLM-as-judge drift — the judge model itself changes (provider update), online quality scores shift systematically without underlying answer-quality changes.
failure-mode.detector: judge model version changed AND composite_score baseline shifted > 5% within 24h
failure-mode.repair: Pin the judge model version explicitly. Reseed baseline scores against the new judge before resuming canary decisions. Document the rebaseline in the rollout plan history.

failure-mode.description: Step promotion under-sampled — engineer promotes the step before min_samples is reached because hold_hours has elapsed; quality signal is too noisy to support the decision.
failure-mode.detector: step promoted at hold_hours expiry AND samples_collected < plan.steps[].min_samples
failure-mode.repair: Promotion gate requires BOTH hold_hours AND min_samples. Extend the step when samples are insufficient; the canary curve's value is in the data accumulation, not in the calendar.

failure-mode.description: Auto-rollback fires on a false signal — judge had a hiccup, kill-switch triggered, real quality was fine, team loses trust in the auto-rollback.
failure-mode.detector: rollback fired AND post-incident review shows the canary version actually outperformed baseline on the offending dimension
failure-mode.repair: Add a 5-minute hold on the kill-switch: trigger fires after the criterion holds for 5 consecutive minutes, with at least 50 samples in window. False-positive analysis updates the criterion: longer window, narrower threshold, or judge-confidence weighting.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Customer or stakeholder pressure to "promote faster, the new version is clearly better" is not a basis for skipping a step. The canary curve is the discipline; subjective conviction is not. Promote only on the data the rollout plan requires.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly: run a rollback game-day. Pick a feature in canary, simulate a quality regression, observe the trigger and rollback path. Measure: trigger latency, completion time, customer-comms time. Any breach of the 60-second contract becomes engineering work for the following quarter.
